milestones.name: Capture + store + view
milestones.outcome: A global docs/shield/backlog.json exists; entries can be added (user + agent) with order, kind, source, and a feature + epic association; /backlog renders the ordered list with per-entry pipeline status from manifest.json; an entry can be manually removed.
exit_criteria: backlog.json has a documented JSON Schema with a top-level schema_version and per-entry {id, order, kind, source, feature, epic, text}; shield/scripts/validate_backlog.py exits 0 on valid and non-zero with a named error on invalid.
An entry can be captured both from the user (/backlog add) and from a Shield skill via a documented write helper; the write is atomic (temp-then-rename).
/backlog renders entries in order with each entry's feature + epic and a research/prd/plan status read from manifest.json.
/backlog can remove an entry by id (plain delete; no retained history).

milestones.name: Feature + epic association + suggestion
milestones.outcome: Every entry references a feature and an epic (existing or proposed-new); the agent suggests a matching feature/epic by scanning manifest.json features and plan.json epics, and the user can accept, pick another, or create-new.
exit_criteria: Capture prompts for (or accepts) a feature + epic; both may be proposed-new.
The agent proposes >=1 candidate feature (from manifest.json) and >=1 candidate epic (from the feature's plan.json) using a documented match; the user can accept/replace/create-new.
Suggestion never blocks capture — an entry can be captured with a proposed-new feature/epic when no match exists.

milestones.name: Promotion + reconciliation
milestones.outcome: The user promotes an entry by starting /research, /prd, /plan, or /implement from it; the entry is removed when its work commits — eagerly at the end of the promoted /plan or /implement run, lazily on the /backlog sweep, or manually. Reconciliation matches by feature (manifest) + epic (plan.json) and never removes on doubt.
exit_criteria: Promoting an entry passes it as a transient reference to /plan or /implement; on success that entry is pruned (eager).
The /backlog sweep removes any entry whose epic's work now appears in the feature's plan.json (lazy safety net); a prd-only feature is NOT removed.
Match key: existing-epic entries match by epic id, proposed-new by epic name; on ambiguity or no match the entry stays.
Eager prune and lazy sweep are idempotent (remove-if-present); reconciliation treats an unrecognized manifest.json/plan.json shape as doubt (entry stays), never crashing.
An executable eval exercises capture, view+status, manual remove, eager prune, lazy sweep, and match-key behavior with a RED->GREEN trail; the Shield plugin version is bumped per CLAUDE.md.

stories.name: Define backlog.json schema and validator
stories.description: Define the docs/shield/backlog.json document shape and a machine-readable JSON Schema with a top-level schema_version, plus a Python validator. Each entry carries id, order (integer), kind (epic|story|task), source (user|agent), feature, epic, and text. schema_version is set now so the open future-scope shape changes (priority buckets, audit trail) can migrate via read-old/write-new.
tasks: Author shield/schema/backlog.schema.json (draft 2020-12) with top-level schema_version (integer) and an entries[] array; entry required fields: id, order, kind, source, feature, epic, text.
Document the entry shape and the schema_version migration policy (read-old/write-new) in a new shield/skills/general/backlog/SKILL.md (or backlog-schema.md).

Create shield/scripts/validate_backlog.py (pydantic + jsonschema, run via uv) — exits 0 on valid, non-zero with a named error (unknown_kind_enum, missing_required_field, schema_version_too_new) on invalid.
Define ordering as a single explicit integer `order` field (no priority buckets in v1).
acceptance_criteria: shield/schema/backlog.schema.json validates a well-formed backlog.json and rejects one with an unknown `kind` value, naming the error.
`uv run shield/scripts/validate_backlog.py <path>` exits 0 on valid sidecars and non-zero with a named error on invalid ones.
backlog.json carries a top-level schema_version; a documented migration policy (read-old/write-new) is present.
kind is constrained to {epic, story, task}; source to {user, agent}; order is an integer.

stories.name: Capture entrypoint (user + skill) with atomic write
stories.description: Provide a capture path usable both by the user (`/backlog add`) and by any Shield skill (a documented write helper). Writes are atomic (temp-then-rename) and validate-or-refuse so a concurrent capture racing reconciliation cannot corrupt backlog.json. This resolves the PRD-review P1 'capture interface undefined'.
tasks: Add a `/backlog add` path to the backlog command that appends an entry (assigns the next `order`).
Expose a skill-callable write helper (documented function/contract: the fields a skill must pass — text, kind, feature?, epic?, source) so an agent can capture mid-task.
Implement atomic write: write backlog.json.tmp then rename; on failure remove .tmp and surface the error.
Validate-or-refuse on read/write: a malformed/partial backlog.json never silently truncates.

LOCKED (plan-review 2026-05-27): write-helper signature `capture(text, *, kind='task', feature=None, epic=None, source) -> entry_id` in shield/scripts/backlog_store.py, raising BacklogInvalid (TRD §11).
LOCKED: concurrency = single-writer assumption (no lock); build full doc -> .tmp -> os.replace(); reads validate-or-refuse (TRD §6 N1).
acceptance_criteria: A user can capture an entry via `/backlog add`; a skill can capture via the documented write helper with source=agent.
The capture interface (command + helper signature/fields) is documented in the backlog SKILL.md.
Killing capture mid-write leaves no corrupted backlog.json (only a .tmp may remain, cleaned up next run).
A new entry is assigned the next integer `order` and a default kind when unspecified.
A malformed/partial backlog.json on read is refused with a named error (BacklogInvalid), never silently read or truncated.

stories.name: /backlog view — ordered list
stories.description: Implement the `/backlog` command + skill that renders the current backlog ordered by the `order` field, showing each entry's feature + epic association and source. Pipeline status badges are added in EPIC-2-S1.
tasks: Author shield/commands/backlog.md and shield/skills/general/backlog/SKILL.md.
Render entries sorted by `order`, each line showing id, feature, epic, source, and text.
Handle the empty-backlog case with a clear message.
acceptance_criteria: `/backlog` lists entries in ascending `order` with feature + epic + source per entry.
An empty backlog renders a clear 'no entries' message, not an error.
The command is registered and discoverable in the Shield plugin.

stories.name: Manual remove from /backlog
stories.description: Allow explicitly removing an entry from `/backlog` by id — for ideas decided against and for entries not cleared by a promotion run. Removal is a plain delete (no retained history in v1).
tasks: Add a `/backlog remove <id>` path that deletes the entry via the atomic write helper.
Confirm-before-delete for a user-initiated remove; surface a clear message if the id is absent.
acceptance_criteria: `/backlog remove <id>` deletes the entry and the change is persisted atomically.
Removing a non-existent id reports a clear error and makes no change.
No history is retained (plain delete), per the v1 decision.

stories.name: Per-entry pipeline status from manifest.json
stories.description: Enrich the `/backlog` view so each entry shows its feature's pipeline status (research / prd / plan) read live from manifest.json artifact flags. This lets an entry show 'prd done, not yet planned' without being removed.
tasks: Read manifest.json; for each entry, look up its feature and surface the artifacts.{research,prd,plan_json} flags as status badges.
Render a badge line per entry (e.g. 'research ✓  prd ✓  plan –'); 'not started' when the feature is absent from manifest.json.
Compute status at view time (no stored status field).
acceptance_criteria: `/backlog` shows research/prd/plan badges per entry derived from manifest.json.
An entry whose feature has a prd but no plan shows 'prd ✓ plan –' and remains in the backlog.
An entry whose feature is absent from manifest.json shows 'not started'.

stories.name: Feature + epic association + agent suggestion
stories.description: At capture/promotion, associate every entry with a feature (the reconciliation key) and an epic (the removal gate) — either may be proposed-new. The agent suggests a matching feature (from manifest.json) and epic (from that feature's plan.json), and the user can accept, pick another, or create-new.
tasks: On capture, prompt for / accept a feature + epic; allow proposed-new for either.
Implement suggestion: scan manifest.json features and the candidate feature's plan.json epics; propose the best match.
Let the user accept the suggestion, choose another, or create-new; never block capture if no match exists.
LOCKED (plan-review 2026-05-27): match = exact normalized (casefold + collapsed whitespace); existing epic by id, proposed-new by exact normalized name; no fuzzy/token-overlap ranking in v1.
acceptance_criteria: Every captured entry carries a feature + epic association (existing or proposed-new).
The agent proposes >=1 feature and >=1 epic candidate when matches exist; the user can accept/replace/create-new.
Capture still succeeds with a proposed-new feature/epic when no match exists.
Suggestion uses exact normalized match only; a 2-way normalized-name tie surfaces both candidates and auto-picks neither.

stories.name: User-driven promotion with transient reference
stories.description: Let the user promote an entry by starting the Shield step they choose (/research, /prd, /plan, or /implement) from it. The promoted command carries the entry id as a transient runtime reference (NOT an id stamped into plan.json), used to prune the entry on success.
tasks: Add a `/backlog promote <id>` affordance that launches the user-chosen step and passes the entry id as a transient promotion reference.
Document that the reference is a runtime command argument, never written into plan.json/story records.
Shippable work routes through /plan; direct /implement stays available for rare planless one-offs.
acceptance_criteria: Promoting an entry starts the user-chosen step and forwards the entry id as a transient reference.
The reference is not persisted into plan.json or any story record (no id stamping).
The user chooses the step; the tool never auto-routes.

stories.name: Reconciliation engine (match key + never-remove-on-doubt)
stories.description: Implement the core reconciliation: locate an entry's feature in manifest.json, and if it has a plan.json, check whether the entry's epic landed. Match key: existing-epic entries by epic id, proposed-new by epic name (names assumed stable). On ambiguity or no match, the entry stays. Treat unrecognized manifest.json/plan.json shapes as doubt (entry stays), never crashing.
tasks: Implement shield/scripts/reconcile_backlog.py: for an entry (feature, epic), read manifest.json (feature present? has plan_json?), then open the flagged plan.json and check for the epic.
Match key: existing epic id match, else proposed-new epic-name match against plan.json epic titles.
Never remove on ambiguity/no-match; a prd-only feature is not removed.
Read-contract drift tolerance: an unrecognized manifest.json/plan.json shape is treated as doubt (entry stays), with a logged warning — no crash.
LOCKED (plan-review 2026-05-27): match key = existing epic by id, proposed-new by casefold+collapsed-ws exact name; tie (>=2 matches) or no match -> entry stays.
acceptance_criteria: An entry whose epic's stories appear in the feature's plan.json is selected for removal; a prd-only feature is not.
Existing-epic entries match by epic id; proposed-new by epic name; ambiguous/no-match entries stay.
A malformed or older-shape manifest.json/plan.json results in 'entry stays' (logged), never an exception.
An entry is removed only when an epic with matching id (existing) or normalized-exact name (proposed-new) is present in plan.json.epics[]; story status is NOT consulted.

stories.name: Eager + lazy removal triggers (idempotent)
stories.description: Wire the two automatic removal triggers on top of the reconciliation engine: eager prune at the end of a promoted /plan or /implement run (using the transient reference), and a lazy sweep on /backlog view. Both are idempotent (remove-if-present) so they can't double-remove or race.
tasks: At the end of /plan and /implement, if the run carried a promotion reference, prune that entry on success (eager).
On /backlog view, run the reconciliation sweep over all entries (lazy safety net).
Make both paths idempotent: removing an already-absent entry is a no-op.
Ensure the sweep and eager prune share the reconciliation engine (no divergent logic).
acceptance_criteria: Promoting via /plan or /implement removes the referenced entry at end of run (eager), before the next /backlog view.
The /backlog sweep removes any entry whose work has since landed in a plan.json (lazy).
Running eager prune then the sweep on the same entry is a no-op the second time (idempotent).
Eager and lazy paths call the same reconciliation engine.

stories.name: Executable evals for the backlog lifecycle (RED→GREEN)
stories.description: Per CLAUDE.md's mandatory eval-coverage policy, author executable evals that exercise the new backlog behavior: capture (user + skill), view + pipeline status, manual remove, eager prune, lazy sweep, match-key, and never-remove-on-doubt. Capture the RED→GREEN paper trail in the PR.
tasks: Author fixtures: a backlog.json + a manifest.json + plan.json set that exercises prd-only-stays, plan-committed-removed, ambiguous-match-stays, and malformed-shape-stays.
Write evals asserting: validate_backlog rejects bad entries; view renders correct status badges; manual remove deletes; eager prune and lazy sweep remove plan-committed entries idempotently.
Wire into the recurring eval runner/CI consistent with existing shield/evals/ patterns.
Capture RED (before) and GREEN (after) outputs in the implementation PR body.
acceptance_criteria: An eval suite under shield/evals/ covers capture, view+status, manual remove, eager prune, lazy sweep, match-key, and never-remove-on-doubt.
Evals are self-contained (no external API/LLM calls) and run via uv.
The PR body contains RED and GREEN sections demonstrating the behavior before and after.
CI runs the eval on PRs touching the backlog assets.

stories.name: Version bump + command/skill docs
stories.description: Ship the feature: bump the Shield plugin version per CLAUDE.md (marketplace.json, and pyproject.toml for any Python package touched) in the same commit as the asset changes, and finalize the /backlog command + backlog SKILL.md docs (capture interface, reconciliation triggers, manual remove).
tasks: Bump .claude-plugin/marketplace.json version for the Shield plugin entry.
Bump pyproject.toml in any Python package modified (e.g. if backlog scripts are packaged).
Finalize shield/commands/backlog.md and shield/skills/general/backlog/SKILL.md: capture interface, three removal triggers, match key, manual remove, status badges.
Update Shield's CHANGELOG noting the backlog feature.
acceptance_criteria: The version is bumped in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (and pyproject.toml where applicable) in the same commit as the asset changes.
/backlog command and backlog SKILL.md document capture, view+status, promote, manual remove, and the three removal triggers.
CHANGELOG mentions the backlog feature.
